channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: El Niño Likely Strongest in 75 Years, US Forecasters Say
item.description: The weather-roiling El Niño that emerged across the Pacific last month has continued to build and will likely be one of the strongest in more than 75 years, the US Climate Prediction Center said.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Suppliers to Top Fashion Brands Face a Rising Climate Challenge
item.description: Manufacturers have a difficult, and costly, task to adapt production lines and protect workers.
item.dc:creator: Azman Usmani, Tracy Withers, Akshat Rathi

item.title: China Climate Plan Pushes Renewables in Factories, Data Centers
item.description: China, the world’s largest source of climate-warming greenhouse gases, plans to more deeply integrate its massive renewable power fleet into factories, data centers and transport in a new plan that lays out more detail of its emissions-cutting strategy through 2030.
item.dc:creator: Lili Pike

item.title: How Trump and AI Data Centers Are Boosting Nuclear Power
item.description: Nuclear energy is back in vogue in the US.
item.dc:creator: Will Wade

item.title: Europe’s Expanding Heat Wave Tests Power Grids and Fire Defenses
item.description: Europe’s third major heat wave this year is set to intensify and expand, extending from west to east over the coming week.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz

item.title: ‘Gargantuan’ Typhoon Risks More Floods in Rain-Soaked China
item.description: China is facing more torrential rain and flooding this weekend from a massive typhoon tracking toward the country’s east coast, following recent severe storms that led to deaths and crop damage.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui, Danny Lee

item.title: EU Plans 2040 Electrification Target to Cut Oil, Gas Consumption
item.description: The European Union wants to propose an electrification goal for the next decade as part of a plan to strengthen the local market for clean technologies and help shift the bloc away from fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska

item.title: UK Grid Issues Another Supply Warning as Heat Tests Network
item.description: The UK grid operator issued another warning over tight electricity supplies for Thursday evening as a heat wave stretching across Europe strains power systems, marking the third such alert this summer.
item.dc:creator: Eamon Farhat, Will Mathis

item.title: Hundreds of Snakes on the Loose After Severe Flooding in China
item.description: The heavy flooding in China’s Guangxi region has created an unusual secondary hazard: hundreds of snakes escaping from inundated breeding farms.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Gu

item.title: Super El Niño Will Give Us a Glimpse of Life in 2035
item.description: Over the next year, the naturally occurring El Niño weather pattern will add to growing damage from climate change.
item.dc:creator: Akshat Rathi, Summer Maxwell
